rationale: Ground truth is a trivial 'lgtm' approve on a pure mechanical rename with no substantive concerns, so recall is vacuous for both. X reaches the same correctly-grounded APPROVE verdict, verifying the rename against the upstream checkout and confirming test updates, though its citation of a specific upstream PR number (vllm#46022) is an unverified embellishment. Y does equally thorough grounding work but then issues 'REQUEST CHANGES' over a single minor test-coverage gap that its own assumptions list admits is 'pre-existing and not a regression from this PR' — a disproportionate, miscalibrated verdict versus the actual state of the PR, even though the underlying suggested test is concrete and actionable.
